investigation_title: Root Cause Investigation — Overhead Crane Wire Rope Failure During Production Lift
investigation_description: The PM programme for OC-001 specified visual inspection only. No bore gauge method or ISO 4309 discard criteria were defined, allowing internal fatigue strand failure to go undetected.

investigation_title: Root Cause Investigation — CNC Spindle Brake Failure — Uncontrolled Rotation After Tool Change
investigation_description: The CNC spindle brake clearance check was absent from the PM procedure. Brake pad wear exceeded manufacturer tolerance undetected across multiple PM cycles.

investigation_title: Root Cause Investigation — HVAC Chiller Failure Causes Clean Room Temperature Exceedance
investigation_description: The HVAC planned maintenance schedule did not include a refrigerant charge inspection for chiller units. No redundant cooling capacity was available for the critical clean room.

investigation_title: Root Cause Investigation — Pressure Vessel Wall Thinning Found at Statutory Inspection
investigation_description: The pressure vessel inspection programme did not account for accelerated corrosion following a process change 18 months prior. Inspection scope was not updated after the change.

investigation_title: Root Cause Investigation — Forklift Hydraulic Hose Burst During Warehouse Stacking Operation
investigation_description: The forklift hydraulic hose replacement schedule used a fixed calendar interval that did not account for elevated wear rates caused by high ambient temperatures at Coimbatore.

investigation_title: Root Cause Investigation — Forklift Operator Near Miss — No Training on Pedestrian Zone Controls
investigation_description: The site pedestrian-vehicle segregation training programme used generic content not specific to the dispatch bay layout. No post-training practical competency assessment was conducted.

investigation_title: Root Cause Investigation — Manual Handler Placed Hands Under Pallet During Unstacking
investigation_description: No task-specific manual handling training was in place for warehouse pickers. Induction covered generic theory only with no pallet stacking task instruction.

investigation_title: Root Cause Investigation — Hot Work Permit Issued by Supervisor Without PTW Authoriser Training
investigation_description: The PTW authoriser role was assigned to all shift supervisors without verifying that each supervisor held the required hot work authoriser training and competency.

investigation_title: Root Cause Investigation — Construction Scaffolder Erected System Type Without Required Certification
investigation_description: The subcontractor induction checklist did not require verification of CISRS card type before allowing operatives to erect scaffolding. Card type was assumed from job title rather than verified.

investigation_title: Root Cause Investigation — Chemical Operator Added Wrong Reagent Due to Inadequate Label Training
investigation_description: Chemical reagent drums used visually similar labels with no colour-coding differentiation. Operator training addressed generic hazard communication but not label disambiguation for this chemical group.

investigation_title: Root Cause Investigation — COSHH Assessment Not Updated After Chemical Substitution
investigation_description: The chemical substitution approval process had no mandatory COSHH reassessment gate. The EHS team was not included in the substitution approval workflow.

investigation_title: Root Cause Investigation — Non-Conforming Components Released Without Deviation Approval
investigation_description: The non-conformance procedure did not specify that a formal deviation approval was required before release of components with dimensional deviations. Supervisors were releasing on personal authority.

investigation_title: Root Cause Investigation — Quality Hold Bypassed — Non-Conforming Units Dispatched to Customer
investigation_description: The quality hold process allowed supervisor override at dispatch without requiring second-level authorisation or recording the override rationale. The control had a single point of failure.

investigation_title: Root Cause Investigation — Management of Change Not Initiated for Temporary Safety System Bypass
investigation_description: The MOC procedure was not communicated to operations supervisors as a mandatory requirement for temporary modifications. The boundary between a temporary measure and a formal change was undefined.

investigation_title: Root Cause Investigation — Group EHS Policy Not Distributed to Four Recently Appointed Site Managers
investigation_description: The Group EHS Policy distribution process relied on site managers self-registering receipt. No push-distribution mechanism or acknowledgement tracking was in place for new manager onboarding.

investigation_title: Root Cause Investigation — Solvent Vapour Overexposure Due to Failed LEV System
investigation_description: LEV ductwork joints in the degreasing booth used push-fit connections subject to vibration-induced separation. Post-maintenance re-test was limited to flow measurement with no duct integrity check.

investigation_title: Root Cause Investigation — Acid Transfer Line Leak at Corroded Flange — Operator Skin Burn
investigation_description: The flange inspection programme for acid-service pipework used a uniform calendar interval. No corrosion rate calculation was performed to assign differentiated intervals based on service severity.

investigation_title: Root Cause Investigation — Flammable Liquid Decanting Without Earth Bonding — Near Miss
investigation_description: The drum decanting SOP did not specify the requirement to earth bond the drum and receiving vessel before opening the drum valve. This step was treated as optional by operators.

investigation_title: Root Cause Investigation — Nitrogen Purge Failure Creates Oxygen-Deficient Atmosphere in Vessel
investigation_description: The nitrogen purge sequence for Reactor R-006 did not require a continuous oxygen monitoring interlock to prevent vessel entry until the atmosphere was confirmed safe. Entry was controlled by procedure only.

investigation_title: Root Cause Investigation — Chemical Storage Bund Capacity Below 110 Percent Regulatory Minimum
investigation_description: Secondary containment for Tank Farm A predated the largest vessel installation and was never recalculated after the capacity upgrade. No periodic bund capacity verification was in the maintenance programme.

investigation_title: Root Cause Investigation — Fire Extinguisher Annual Service Overdue — Six Units Out of Date
investigation_description: The annual fire extinguisher service was managed by a single contractor whose appointment lapsed without a renewal alert. No internal due-date tracking existed for legally-required contractor services.

investigation_title: Root Cause Investigation — Emergency Exit Partially Obstructed by Stored Pallet Stacks
investigation_description: The daily fire escape route inspection checklist did not include a check for clear floor path to emergency exits. Pallet placement by operations staff was not classified as an obstruction risk.

investigation_title: Root Cause Investigation — Emergency Lighting Battery Failures Found on Annual Duration Test
investigation_description: The emergency lighting battery replacement programme used a fixed 5-year cycle that did not account for shorter battery service life in high-ambient-temperature areas of the electronics plant.

investigation_title: Root Cause Investigation — Sprinkler Quarterly Flow Test Overdue — No Deferral Approval Recorded
investigation_description: The sprinkler maintenance schedule was in a paper logbook not reviewed during a responsible-person handover. The quarterly test fell due during a period when no named responsible person was in post.

investigation_title: Root Cause Investigation — Emergency Assembly Point Signs Removed During Construction — No Replacement
investigation_description: The hot works permit did not require re-erection of emergency assembly point signage before permit closure. The construction method statement did not identify assembly point visibility as a works requirement.

investigation_title: Root Cause Investigation — OC-001 Wire Rope Broken Strands Found — First Documented Recurrence
investigation_description: Wire rope inspection was performed visually only. Training-only corrective action provided as sole remedy did not address the absence of a technical inspection standard for internal rope condition.

investigation_title: Root Cause Investigation — OC-001 Wire Rope Below Discard Criteria — Second Recurrence
investigation_description: Training-only corrective action from prior CAPA did not change inspection method or interval. No reduction in degradation rate was achieved. Absence of discard criteria remained unaddressed.

investigation_title: Root Cause Investigation — OC-001 Wire Rope Degraded to Failure Before Scheduled Replacement — Third
investigation_description: PM interval shortened from 30 to 21 days but no inspection technique improvement was made. Discard criteria still absent. Prior CAPA actions recorded as ineffective at effectiveness check.

investigation_title: Root Cause Investigation — OC-001 Wire Rope Management Failure — Fourth Documented Occurrence
investigation_description: Fourth occurrence confirms systemic failure of corrective programme. Inspection technique, discard criteria, and PM frequency each addressed in isolation across prior CAPAs but never as an integrated system.

investigation_title: Root Cause Investigation — OC-001 Crane Wire Rope Management System Overhaul — Active CAPA
investigation_description: Fault tree analysis identified absence of condition-based wire rope management as the systemic root cause. Prior CAPAs addressed symptoms without rebuilding the programme to ISO 4309 standard.

investigation_title: Root Cause Investigation — Forklift-Pedestrian Near Miss at Dispatch Bay B — First Occurrence
investigation_description: Forklift operators received no training specific to the dispatch bay layout. Classroom induction covered generic pedestrian awareness only. No competency test was conducted.

investigation_title: Root Cause Investigation — Forklift Near Miss at Pedestrian Zone — Second Occurrence Same Location
investigation_description: Classroom training delivered after first CAPA but no behavioural competency assessment was conducted. Training records were signed but practical driving behaviour was not observed or verified.

investigation_title: Root Cause Investigation — Forklift Reversed Into Pedestrian Walkway — Third Occurrence
investigation_description: Refresher training from second CAPA focused on knowledge recall rather than behaviour change. No physical controls introduced. Training-only approach proven ineffective across two prior CAPAs.

investigation_title: Root Cause Investigation — Forklift Pedestrian Conflict — Fourth Occurrence Despite Three Prior CAPAs
investigation_description: Four near misses at the same location confirm training-only actions cannot control this hazard. Root cause is absence of physical pedestrian segregation, not an operator knowledge deficit.

investigation_title: Root Cause Investigation — Forklift Pedestrian Segregation Programme Overhaul — Active CAPA
investigation_description: Bow-tie analysis confirmed absence of physical pedestrian segregation as the primary control gap. Training and signage are inadequate as sole controls for a forklift-pedestrian conflict in an active dispatch area.

investigation_title: Root Cause Investigation — LTI: Welder Sustains Arc Flash Burns — No Arc Flash Risk Assessment
investigation_description: The live panel works procedure did not specify arc flash boundary distance or require arc flash PPE. The risk assessment classified the task as low voltage with no arc flash assessment conducted.

investigation_title: Root Cause Investigation — LTI: Worker Arm Fracture — Crane Load Swing Without Lift Plan
investigation_description: The crane and lifting procedure did not require a written lift plan for lifts below 500 kg. No exclusion zone establishment requirement existed. Workers approached the load path without restriction.

investigation_title: Root Cause Investigation — LTI: Worker Struck by Reversing Excavator on Construction Site
investigation_description: The site traffic management plan did not designate the excavation zone as a plant-exclusion area for pedestrians. Banksman duties were unassigned during the reverse manoeuvre phase of the operation.

investigation_title: Root Cause Investigation — ISO 45001 Internal Audit Finds Management Review Not Documented
investigation_description: The ISO 45001 management review procedure specified a review must be conducted annually but did not define minimum outputs, required agenda items, or record format. Reviews were conducted informally.

investigation_title: Root Cause Investigation — NCR: CAPA Closure Rate Below 80 Percent — Customer Audit Finding
investigation_description: No escalation process existed for CAPAs approaching or exceeding their target closure dates. CAPA owners were not prompted when due dates were missed and overdue items had no management visibility.
